news.title:
Sensex fell 2.25%, Nifty 50 dropped 2.09%, eroding ₹9 lakh crore market cap as 906 stocks hit 52-week lows.
Stocks like HAL, Tata Motors, IDBI Bank, and UCO Bank hit lows; near-term sentiment fragile amid geopolitical risks.
news.summary:
Sensex fell 2.25%, Nifty 50 dropped 2.09%, eroding ₹9 lakh crore market cap as 906 stocks hit 52-week lows.
Stocks like HAL, Tata Motors, IDBI Bank, and UCO Bank hit lows; near-term sentiment fragile amid geopolitical risks.

news.title:
PSU banks, including Canara Bank, UCO Bank, and SBI, fell up to 5% due to rising bond yields reaching 6.9%.
The Nifty PSU Bank index dropped 3.6%, with 7 stocks declining 10-21% in 2026, led by Punjab & Sind Bank (-21%).
news.summary:
PSU banks, including Canara Bank, UCO Bank, and SBI, fell up to 5% due to rising bond yields reaching 6.9%.
The Nifty PSU Bank index dropped 3.6%, with 7 stocks declining 10-21% in 2026, led by Punjab & Sind Bank (-21%).

news.title:
UCO Bank's valuation shifts to 'attractive' from 'very attractive,' signaling re-rating potential.
Improved P/E and P/B ratios highlight price attractiveness despite underperformance vs. Sensex.
news.summary:
UCO Bank's valuation shifts to 'attractive' from 'very attractive,' signaling re-rating potential.
Improved P/E and P/B ratios highlight price attractiveness despite underperformance vs. Sensex.

news.title:
Nifty PSU Bank index falls for fourth day, down 0.6%, impacted by rising bond yields and crude oil prices.
March losses reach 14%, with Punjab & Sind Bank, Indian Overseas Bank, UCO Bank hitting 52-week lows.
news.summary:
Nifty PSU Bank index falls for fourth day, down 0.6%, impacted by rising bond yields and crude oil prices.
March losses reach 14%, with Punjab & Sind Bank, Indian Overseas Bank, UCO Bank hitting 52-week lows.

news.title:
UCO Bank shares dropped 8.2%, underperforming Sensex's 3% decline by week ending 6 March.
Stock hit 52-week low, showing mixed momentum later; fundamentals and 'Hold' rating indicate nuanced outlook.
news.summary:
UCO Bank shares dropped 8.2%, underperforming Sensex's 3% decline by week ending 6 March.
Stock hit 52-week low, showing mixed momentum later; fundamentals and 'Hold' rating indicate nuanced outlook.

news.title:
IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers resigns; Rahul Bhatia takes interim charge. HG Infra wins ₹401.3 crore railway project in Madhya Pradesh.
Waaree Energies invests ₹30 million in U.S. solar firm. Brigade Enterprises launches industrial park in Bengaluru; UCO Bank keeps MCLR unchanged.
news.summary:
IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers resigns; Rahul Bhatia takes interim charge. HG Infra wins ₹401.3 crore railway project in Madhya Pradesh.
Waaree Energies invests ₹30 million in U.S. solar firm. Brigade Enterprises launches industrial park in Bengaluru; UCO Bank keeps MCLR unchanged.

news.title:
UCO Bank updates benchmark rates; MCLR remains unchanged with rates from 7.90% to 8.75% effective 10.03.2026.
TBLR 3-month rate revised to 5.30% and G-Sec 1-year rate to 5.58%; other benchmark rates unchanged.
news.summary:
UCO Bank updates benchmark rates; MCLR remains unchanged with rates from 7.90% to 8.75% effective 10.03.2026.
TBLR 3-month rate revised to 5.30% and G-Sec 1-year rate to 5.58%; other benchmark rates unchanged.

news.title:
RBI fined UCO Bank ₹38.6 lakh for non-compliance with savings interest, locker rent, and SHG credit reporting rules.
The violations were identified during a 2025 inspection. UCO Bank is improving processes to prevent future issues.
news.summary:
RBI fined UCO Bank ₹38.6 lakh for non-compliance with savings interest, locker rent, and SHG credit reporting rules.
The violations were identified during a 2025 inspection. UCO Bank is improving processes to prevent future issues.

news.title:
UCO Bank announced an Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) on 16th March 2026 via video conferencing.
Key agenda includes the election of a Shareholder Director and extension of Shri Rajendra Kumar Saboo's tenure as Executive Director.
news.summary:
UCO Bank announced an Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) on 16th March 2026 via video conferencing.
Key agenda includes the election of a Shareholder Director and extension of Shri Rajendra Kumar Saboo's tenure as Executive Director.

news.title:
ED attaches Rs 1.2 crore assets in UCO Bank fraud case.
32 fake gold loans across 3 branches linked to accused individuals.
news.summary:
ED attaches Rs 1.2 crore assets in UCO Bank fraud case.
32 fake gold loans across 3 branches linked to accused individuals.

news.title:
UCO Bank revises TBLR and G-Sec linked rates effective 11.02.2026.
MCLR, Repo Linked Rate, Base Rate, and BPLR remain unchanged.
news.summary:
UCO Bank revises TBLR and G-Sec linked rates effective 11.02.2026.
MCLR, Repo Linked Rate, Base Rate, and BPLR remain unchanged.

news.title:
UCO Bank's business grew 13.3% YoY, driven by 10.6% deposit and 16.7% advances growth.
Net profit rose 15.7%, NIM improved to 3.1%, and Gross NPA reduced to 2.4%.
news.summary:
UCO Bank's business grew 13.3% YoY, driven by 10.6% deposit and 16.7% advances growth.
Net profit rose 15.7%, NIM improved to 3.1%, and Gross NPA reduced to 2.4%.

news.title:
PSU banks like Punjab & Sind Bank, UCO Bank, Bank of Maharashtra, IOB, and Central Bank of India reported strong Q3FY26 results, driven by profit growth and rising net interest income.
Key highlights: IOB's net profit surged 56%, Bank of Maharashtra's by 27%, and Central Bank of India's by 31%, boosting investor sentiment.
news.summary:
PSU banks like Punjab & Sind Bank, UCO Bank, Bank of Maharashtra, IOB, and Central Bank of India reported strong Q3FY26 results, driven by profit growth and rising net interest income.
Key highlights: IOB's net profit surged 56%, Bank of Maharashtra's by 27%, and Central Bank of India's by 31%, boosting investor sentiment.

news.title:
UCO Bank reports a potential strike by UFBU on 27th Jan 2026.
Bank ensures measures for smooth operations but warns of possible disruptions.
news.summary:
UCO Bank reports a potential strike by UFBU on 27th Jan 2026.
Bank ensures measures for smooth operations but warns of possible disruptions.

news.title: UCO Bank plans 10-14% business growth, raising Rs 2,700 Cr via QIP.
news.summary: UCO Bank plans 10-14% business growth, raising Rs 2,700 Cr via QIP.

news.title:
UCO Bank seeks SEBI extension for dilution as govt holds 90.95% stake, delays meeting 25% public shareholding deadline.
Bank reports 15.65% rise in Q3 profit to Rs 739 crore, strong operational growth, improved margins, and better asset quality.
news.summary:
UCO Bank seeks SEBI extension for dilution as govt holds 90.95% stake, delays meeting 25% public shareholding deadline.
Bank reports 15.65% rise in Q3 profit to Rs 739 crore, strong operational growth, improved margins, and better asset quality.

news.title:
UCO Bank's Q3 GNPA projected at 2.4%, down from 2.6% QoQ.
NNPA for Q3 is expected to decline to 0.4% from 0.4% QoQ.
news.summary:
UCO Bank's Q3 GNPA projected at 2.4%, down from 2.6% QoQ.
NNPA for Q3 is expected to decline to 0.4% from 0.4% QoQ.

news.title:
UCO Bank's Q3 net profit rose to ₹7.4B from ₹6.4B YoY.
Q3 interest earned increased to ₹66.5B compared to ₹62.2B YoY.
news.summary:
UCO Bank's Q3 net profit rose to ₹7.4B from ₹6.4B YoY.
Q3 interest earned increased to ₹66.5B compared to ₹62.2B YoY.

news.title:
UCO Bank's board to meet on 17 Jan 2026 to approve unaudited financial results for Q3 and nine months ending Dec 2025.
Trading window for insiders remains closed from 1 Jan 2026 until 48 hours post results declaration.
news.summary:
UCO Bank's board to meet on 17 Jan 2026 to approve unaudited financial results for Q3 and nine months ending Dec 2025.
Trading window for insiders remains closed from 1 Jan 2026 until 48 hours post results declaration.

news.title:
Indian banks' market cap surged in Q3FY26 driven by GST cuts and festive demand, with smaller private banks leading the gains.
HDFC Bank rose 4.4% to Rs 15,249.2B, SBI gained 12.6% to Rs 9,066.3B, while IDFC First Bank saw 43.8% growth to Rs 735.9B.
news.summary:
Indian banks' market cap surged in Q3FY26 driven by GST cuts and festive demand, with smaller private banks leading the gains.
HDFC Bank rose 4.4% to Rs 15,249.2B, SBI gained 12.6% to Rs 9,066.3B, while IDFC First Bank saw 43.8% growth to Rs 735.9B.
